message:
test(fj-runtime): snapshot frankentui status card rendering under frankentui-integration feature

Add an insta snapshot test for the frankentui StatusCard renderer
under the feature-gated frankentui-integration code path. The
status card is what fj-runtime emits to the FrankenJAX TUI dashboard
when a session is running; its formatting is part of the
user-visible UI surface, so any unintentional change (header
position, mode label casing, percent formatting) should fail loudly
in CI rather than silently shipping.

crates/fj-runtime/Cargo.toml
- Add `insta = "1.47.2"` to [dev-dependencies]. Insta is already
  used elsewhere in the workspace (ft-serialize) at the same
  version, so the lockfile resolution stays stable.

crates/fj-runtime/src/lib.rs
- frankentui_status_card_rendering_matches_snapshot: gated on
  `#[cfg(feature = "frankentui-integration")]` so default builds
  don't pull insta or the frankentui_bridge module. Constructs a
  StatusCard with title="FrankenJAX Runtime", mode="hardened",
  confidence_percent=87, runs render_status_card, and pipes the
  output through insta::assert_snapshot! with the explicit name
  "frankentui_status_card" so the snapshot file lives at
  src/snapshots/fj_runtime__tests__frankentui_status_card.snap
  rather than a generated name based on the test fn.

The first run after `cargo insta accept` blesses the current
output; subsequent runs assert byte-for-byte equality against the
blessed snapshot.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
